a:r.a:t: Config.py is the central nervous system for tuning the entire platform, with over 300 configuration variables. Every number on this slide is a default that can be overridden via environment variables. Context budget management controls how much of the LLM's context window gets used: context_window_tokens is 32,768 by default, context_target_ratio of 0.72 means we aim to use 72 percent of the window leaving headroom for the response, and autocompact_threshold of 0.85 triggers automatic context compaction when usage exceeds 85 percent. Tool result limits cap individual tool outputs at 2,000 tokens and total tool results per turn at 8,000 tokens, preventing any single tool from consuming the entire context. The worker scheduler uses weighted fair queuing with three priority lanes: urgent tasks get weight 8, interactive tasks get weight 3, and background tasks get weight 1. Per-tenant token budgets prevent one heavy user from starving others - 24,000 tokens per minute with burst allowance of 48,000, and a token cost factor of 1.35x accounts for overhead. RAG defaults include top_k of 15 for both vector and keyword retrieval, chunk size 900 with 150 overlap, Reciprocal Rank Fusion k of 60 for merging results, 2 retries on insufficient evidence, minimum 2 evidence chunks, and 4 parallel collection probes. Memory retrieves up to 16 candidate memories within a 1,600-character budget. These defaults represent production-tuned values - change them carefully.
